title: Fix --layers ADD from url issue
body:
When building with layers and using the ADD command to
add a file using a url, the build process would get stuck
in the second round when using the cache. This was happening
because the node traversal code was not moving on to the next
node when a url was detected hence causing an infinite loop.

Fixes https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/1276
Once this gets into buildah, will vendor it into libpod for podman as well.

Signed-off-by: umohnani8 <umohnani@redhat.com>

comments.body:
@rhatdan @TomSweeneyRedHat PTAL.
I want to add a test, but I am not sure what remote file to use that I know will exist for a long time - don't want the test suite failing in future because the url doesn't exist anymore.

Red Hat CI is failing with `Error copying image to the remote destination: Error checking for blob sha256:73046094a9b835e443af1a9d736fcfc11a994107500e474d0abf399499ed280c at destination: Error checking whether a blob sha256:73046094a9b835e443af1a9d736fcfc11a994107500e474d0abf399499ed280c exists in localhost:5000/my-alpine: unauthorized: authentication required`.
Any idea why we are getting unauthorized?
@umohnani8 more info in my status that just went out, I'm investigating.  Short answer, not a clue ATM and it's unexpected (and unrelated to your PR).

@umohnani8 How about pulling README.md from this git repository.
https://github.com/projectatomic/buildah/blob/master/README.md

Added commit to fix https://github.com/projectatomic/buildah/issues/949 as well.
@umohnani8 my PR just went in, I think this will work with a rebase and push.
Ugh, look like more fedora registry ills in the papr test.
